fields.description:
# Agentic code-review capability for rebar (off by default, source-separated), dogfooded via the Gerrit LLM-Review gate

## Context
Build the agentic code-review capability as a SEPARATE, OFF-BY-DEFAULT rebar capability whose source is clearly isolated so it has no effect when disabled. It uses the three-pass review structure (evidence -> binary-verify -> deterministic gate) and is enforced through Gerrit's `LLM-Review` label on the AWS-hosted environment. Dogfooding on rebar itself is the success criterion.

## Prerequisite foundations — current codebase state (2026-06)

Three foundations shipped since this epic was written; the design is unchanged, but the substrate is now
CONCRETE for some parts and still PENDING for others (the closed prerequisite epics are NOT reopened — this
section realigns THIS epic to what they delivered):

- **SHIPPED — native `review_code` engine.** `src/rebar/llm/code_review.py` (`review_code`), the write-gated
  MCP `review_code` tool, and `rebar review-code --base.. --head` already exist: deterministic reviewer
  SELECTION by `applies_to` globs → parallel reviewer runs → `aggregate_findings` (cluster by file+dimension,
  consensus, severity×agreement rank) → a `review_result`. NOTE: today it is SINGLE-PASS (evidence +
  aggregation), NOT yet three-pass.
- **SHIPPED — provider-agnostic runner.** The runner is now `PydanticAIRunner` (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py`),
  provider-agnostic by model-string prefix (claude/gpt/gemini) and the sole runtime (LangGraph cut over per
  `e6d9`); the `execution_mode` {single_turn|agentic} seam is honored.
- **SHIPPED — contract-bearing reviewer prompts (epic `da27`, closed).** The CODE reviewers `code_quality` /
  `security` / `tests` are front-matter prompts (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/*.md`) carrying `inputs`/`outputs`
  contracts, `execution_mode`, `dimension`, and `applies_to` globs (e.g. `security.md` → `**/auth*`,
  `**/*secret*`, `**/security/**`, `**/signing*`), with a derived index + CI drift gate; the workflow engine
  (`e6d9`) + the Inspect-AI eval seam back them.
- **SHIPPED substrate, detectors PENDING — code-grounding oracle (epic `8f6c`, closed).** `src/rebar/grounding/`
  ships the fail-open evidence oracle + an EXTENSIBLE detector registry (`detectors/registry.py`; built-in +
  project `.rebar/detectors/`), the substrate for Pass-2 code-grounding. BUT it ships only `smell_generic` +
  applicability detectors today — there are NO secrets / High-Critical-security detectors yet, so the
  high-precision auto-block below has the registry as its substrate while its specific detectors are NEW WORK
  here (add detectors, or wrap an external scanner — gitleaks/semgrep — behind the oracle's detector envelope).
- **STILL PENDING (this epic's own work):** the THREE-PASS pipeline (Pass-2 verifier + Pass-3 deterministic
  gate) — `review_code` is single-pass today and the framework is the OPEN epic `9da1` this depends_on;
  SOURCE-SEPARATION (review_code is currently INLINE in `llm/code_review.py`, env-gated by
  `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` but not isolated); and the GERRIT `LLM-Review` voter wiring (no Gerrit integration
  exists in-repo — see the AWS/Gerrit provisioning prerequisite epic `d251`).

## Success Criteria
- Code review runs as the rebar MCP server's `LLM-Review` voter in Gerrit, off by default and source-isolated (inert and clearly non-applicable when the feature flag is off).
- rebar's own changes are reviewed and gated by it end-to-end through the Gerrit gate (dogfood).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Capability is OFF by default (config/env gated) and SOURCE-SEPARATED into its own module(s), so the code path is inert and unmistakably non-applicable when disabled. (Today `review_code` is env-gated via `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` but lives INLINE in `src/rebar/llm/code_review.py`; this epic isolates it into its own optional module — PRESERVING the existing public surface: the MCP `review_code` tool and the `rebar review-code` CLI keep working via re-export/shim, so isolation does not break current callers.)
- [ ] Migrate `review_code` from its current SINGLE-PASS shape (evidence + `aggregate_findings`) onto the three-pass framework (pass-1 evidence -> pass-2 verifier agent -> pass-3 deterministic typed gate) — that framework is built in the reviewer-migration epic `9da1` (OPEN; this epic depends_on it). The migration also RETIRES the single-pass path (no `evidence + aggregate_findings`-only route remains reachable) — a half-migration where single-pass code still runs does NOT satisfy this.
- [ ] BUILT ON the SHIPPED substrate (PRECONDITIONS, not deliverables): the provider-agnostic `PydanticAIRunner` (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py`) + native `review_code` (`src/rebar/llm/code_review.py` + MCP `review_code` + `rebar review-code`). The DELIVERABLE here is wiring the three-pass code-review CAPABILITY onto them (provider-agnostic via the runner's model-string dispatch); an optional PR-Agent runner may sit behind the runner seam but is not required.
- [ ] Wired as the Gerrit `LLM-Review` voter: on patchset-created, review the change, run pass-1/2/3, and vote `LLM-Review` via REST; the deterministic block/advisory outcome maps to the label.
- [ ] Hard auto-block reserved for high-precision categories (secrets / High-Critical security) — sourced from grounding-oracle detectors (epic `8f6c`'s detector registry is the substrate; the secrets/security detectors are NEW WORK here: add them, or wrap gitleaks/semgrep behind the oracle's detector envelope); everything else advisory at the starting (high) thresholds. CRITICAL: the hard auto-block must NOT inherit the oracle's general fail-open posture — if a secrets/security detector input is UNAVAILABLE or errors, the auto-block category FAILS CLOSED (or surfaces an explicit coverage gap on the vote), so a high-precision block is never SILENTLY skipped (fail-open-for-coverage applies to ADVISORY grounding, not to the hard BLOCK decision).
- [ ] Dogfood proof: at least one real rebar change is reviewed + gated end-to-end on the AWS/Gerrit environment.
